# Editable

An inline editable text component that allows users to click on text to edit it in place. Composed of multiple sub-components for preview, input, and control triggers.

## Import

```jsx
// Nested property pattern (recommended)
import { Editable } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";

// Usage: <Editable.Preview />, <Editable.Input />, etc.

// Named imports (backwards compatible)
import {
  Editable,
  EditablePreview,
  EditableInput,
  EditableTextarea,
  EditableControl,
  EditableEditTrigger,
  EditableSubmitTrigger,
  EditableCancelTrigger,
} from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
```

## Sub-components

| Component | Nested Property | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Editable` | - | The root wrapper (renders `Editable.Root`). Manages editing state and value |
| `EditablePreview` | `Editable.Preview` | The non-editing display text (click to edit) |
| `EditableInput` | `Editable.Input` | The single-line input shown in edit mode |
| `EditableTextarea` | `Editable.Textarea` | A multi-line textarea shown in edit mode |
| `EditableControl` | `Editable.Control` | Container for the edit, submit, and cancel triggers |
| `EditableEditTrigger` | `Editable.EditTrigger` | Button to enter edit mode |
| `EditableSubmitTrigger` | `Editable.SubmitTrigger` | Button to confirm changes |
| `EditableCancelTrigger` | `Editable.CancelTrigger` | Button to discard changes |

## Editable (Root) Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `defaultValue` | `string` | - | Initial value for uncontrolled usage |
| `value` | `string` | - | Controlled value |
| `onValueChange` | `(details: { value: string }) => void` | - | Callback fired when the value changes |
| `onValueCommit` | `(details: { value: string }) => void` | - | Callback fired when the edit is submitted |
| `onValueRevert` | `(details: { value: string }) => void` | - | Callback fired when the edit is cancelled |
| `placeholder` | `string` | - | Placeholder text shown when value is empty |
| `activationMode` | `"click" \| "dblclick" \| "focus" \| "none"` | `"click"` | How the edit mode is activated |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether the editable is disabled |
| `ref` | `React.Ref` | - | Forwarded ref to the root element |

## Usage

### Basic with input

```jsx
// Nested property pattern (recommended)
<Editable defaultValue="Click to edit">
  <Editable.Preview />
  <Editable.Input />
</Editable>

// Named imports (backwards compatible)
<Editable defaultValue="Click to edit">
  <EditablePreview />
  <EditableInput />
</Editable>
```

### With textarea

```jsx
<Editable defaultValue="Click to edit this longer text...">
  <Editable.Preview />
  <Editable.Textarea />
</Editable>
```

### With control buttons

```jsx
<Editable defaultValue="Edit me">
  <Editable.Preview />
  <Editable.Input />
  <Editable.Control>
    <Editable.EditTrigger>Edit</Editable.EditTrigger>
    <Editable.SubmitTrigger>Save</Editable.SubmitTrigger>
    <Editable.CancelTrigger>Cancel</Editable.CancelTrigger>
  </Editable.Control>
</Editable>
```

### With double-click activation

```jsx
<Editable defaultValue="Double-click to edit" activationMode="dblclick">
  <Editable.Preview />
  <Editable.Input />
</Editable>
```

### Controlled

```jsx
const [value, setValue] = useState("Hello World");

<Editable
  value={value}
  onValueChange={(e) => setValue(e.value)}
  onValueCommit={(e) => console.log("Saved:", e.value)}
>
  <Editable.Preview />
  <Editable.Input />
</Editable>
```

## Features

- Inline editing with seamless preview-to-input transition
- Supports both single-line input and multi-line textarea
- Multiple activation modes: click, double-click, focus, or programmatic
- Submit and cancel triggers for explicit save/discard actions
- Controlled and uncontrolled value management
- Each sub-component forwards refs to its underlying DOM element
